CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Produce Deterministic, Reproducible Behaviour for Audit
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Runtime Security**. Verifies that each AI agent is designed to produce deterministic and reproducible behaviour. Non-deterministic agents cannot be reliably audited, replayed for incident investigation, or compared against a known-good baseline. Determinism requires fixed random seeds, pinned model versions, and idempotent tool implementations.
Risk: Non-deterministic agents produce different outputs for identical inputs, making it impossible to reproduce a specific sequence of actions for forensic analysis after a security incident. Regulators and auditors increasingly require that AI system decisions be explainable and reproducible. Non-determinism also makes automated testing unreliable, allowing regressions in safety controls to go undetected.

Code.Other:
1. Set temperature=0 (or equivalent) and a fixed seed in all LLM API calls to maximise output consistency.
2. Pin the exact model version in API calls (e.g., gpt-4o-2024-11-20, not gpt-4o) to prevent silent model updates from changing behaviour.
3. Design agent tools to be idempotent: the same input should always produce the same state change.
4. Log full request/response payloads (with masking for PII) to enable replay and audit of past decisions.

Recommendation.Text: Set temperature=0 and pin exact model versions in all LLM calls. Design agent tools to be idempotent and log complete request/response payloads to enable forensic replay.

Notes: ASPM-065 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
